title: Trump’s Push for Electoral Retribution Heads to the Ballot Box
summary: The president’s push to punish political enemies in his own party will play a starring role in a series of Republican primaries this month in which he has backed challengers.

title: These G.O.P. Women Want Congress Members to Pay a Price for Abuse. Will It Work?
summary: Three Republican women who had a hand in forcing the resignations of lawmakers accused of sexual misconduct said they are out to name and shame more offenders. It is not clear how far they will go.

title: Eric Swalwell Was Proficient in Social Media. So Were His Accusers.
summary: Eric Swalwell used social media to boost his image and meet women, accusers said. Some of those same women used apps and influencers to join forces years later.

title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
summary: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

title: How Germany May Have Misjudged Trump’s Anger on Iran
summary: After Chancellor Friedrich Merz upset President Trump with criticism of the war, he offered no public sign he believed Mr. Trump’s threats to pull troops were serious.

title: Hegseth’s and Moulton’s Parallel Lives Collide Over Iran
summary: The defense secretary and Representative Seth Moulton, a Democrat, debated the Iran war through the prism of their shared experiences fighting Iraq.

title: Not Even Japanese Bathhouses Are Immune From Shocks of Iran War
summary: High fuel costs are imperiling the Japanese sento, a long-declining industry that remains a vital social lifeline for the country’s isolated elderly.

title: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down After Years of Struggle
summary: Spirit once upended the industry by offering very low fares but was in its second bankruptcy in two years after years of struggle.

title: Spirit Airlines is Shutting Down. Here’s What to Do if You’re Scheduled to Fly.
summary: After two stints in bankruptcy, the low-cost airline shut down early Saturday. Here’s what travelers should know about rebooking and refunds.

title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
summary: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

title: Kennedy Consulted With Scandal P.R. Firm While Seeking Cabinet Nomination
summary: An associate of the firm, which has been accused of running a smear campaign against the actress Blake Lively, promised to suppress negative stories about Robert F. Kennedy Jr., records show.

title: N.I.H. Reinstates Employee Put on Leave After Criticizing Trump Research Cuts
summary: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.

title: Tucker Carlson Discusses Breaking With Trump, the Iran War and Antisemitism
summary: The conservative media commentator split with the administration over the war in Iran. Will the breakup last?

title: Takeaways From The Times’s Interview With Tucker Carlson
summary: He spoke about the rupture with President Trump and what it portends for the  conservative movement. Here are the key moments from our conversation.

title: How We Chose What to Ask and When to Challenge Tucker Carlson
summary: “The Interview” host Lulu Garcia-Navarro talks about her preparation to interview Mr. Carlson and some judgment calls she had to make in real time.

title: Can a Second-Home Tax Work in New York? The Numbers Don’t Add Up Yet.
summary: The tax proposal is being held up as a generational attempt to make the ultrawealthy pay more to society and potentially raise $500 million a year for New York City.

title: Do Higher Taxes Drive the Rich Away? Mamdani’s Plan Revives the Question.
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani wants to raise income taxes on New York City’s millionaires. Tax increases elsewhere have not led to an exodus, but some worry the city is reaching a tipping point.

title: Ashley McBryde Saved Her Own Life. Now She’s Rocking Out Her Way.
summary: The prolific country singer and songwriter has never quite fit the country music mold. Her upcoming album, “Wild,” revels in her individuality, and her sobriety.

title: As Kentucky Derby Becomes a Bigger Business, Locals Feel Left Behind
summary: Locals say rising ticket prices and a late start time for the Oaks race on Friday are hurting residents and restaurants. “The only thing I see is greed,” one steakhouse owner said.

title: His Majesty and Our Travesty
summary: The real king delivers a needed royal lesson on democracy.

title: Slouching Toward Kamala Harris
summary: The Democrats’ fundamental condition is a late-Trumpian stasis.

title: Public Offering
summary: How often do you talk to strangers? What’s stopping you?

title: Mexican Governor Accused by U.S. of Aiding Drug Cartel Steps Down
summary: U.S. prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya of protecting the powerful Sinaloa Cartel in exchange for bribes and political support, setting off a national scandal.

title: After Trump’s Ousting of Maduro, Little Has Changed for Most Venezuelans
summary: U.S. officials say they will “unleash prosperity” by commandeering the oil industry. Many people in Caracas say it will take far more than that.

title: UK Driving-Test Backlog Leaves Learners Waiting Months
summary: The average wait for a test in England and Scotland has hit 22 weeks, up from five weeks before the pandemic, leading to frustration and attempts to jump the line.
